data.title: Fitts's Law
data.description: Interface design that accounts for users taking action quickly by optimizing button size and placement.

data.title: Hick's Law
data.description: Minimized choices on the interface to reduce decision time and improve user experience.

data.title: Miller’s Law
data.description: Users can only retain up to 7 items in their working memory, so menus and options are grouped logically.
data.technical_spec: Logical grouping of playlists, categorized music recommendations.

data.title: Jakob's Law
data.description: Being familiar with user expectations based on similar platforms to enhance usability.

data.title: Aesthetic-Usability Effect
data.description: Emphasis on aesthetic design to create positive first impressions and perceived usability.
